name: Comparison — RCT vs Observational Study Design (4 attributes)
input: Compare Randomized Controlled Trial vs Observational Study. RCT: randomization eliminates confounding, gold standard for causality, expensive ($10M-$100M), ethical limitations on interventions. Observational: real-world generalizability, lower cost ($100K-$5M), larger sample sizes possible, susceptible to confounding bias.
qualityCriteria: Description for each item is present
No placeholder or lorem ipsum content
napkinBenchmark: Two-column table with pro/con attributes listed side by side. Headers in bold with distinct background colors.

data.title: RCT vs Observational Study Design

items.label: Randomized Controlled Trial
items.description: Randomization eliminates confounding; gold standard for causality; costly ($10M-$100M); ethical limitations on harmful interventions

items.label: Observational Study
items.description: Real-world generalizability; lower cost ($100K-$5M); larger sample sizes feasible; susceptible to confounding bias

data.caption: Trade-offs between interventional and observational research designs
